my_thoughts: The assertion/definition works: payment = central (fundamental meta-rule), defines: practical study (pay regardless of everything else), links: refusal = mechanicalness (mechanical reaction). The student requested protocol (seeking practical mechanism), which shows they understand: paying in advance = needs explanation (confusion). They'll likely accept the assertion/definition. The episode is complete: negotiation → denial/consequence → mechanism → assertion/definition. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student questions term (seeking to negotiate cost)—denial/consequence: no negotiation, zero result. T2: Student requested protocol ('I do not understand about paying in advance. How is it done?')—seeking practical mechanism via admits confusion and requests protocol. They understand: paying in advance = needs explanation (confusion). The ontological_shift mechanism asserts: payment = central (fundamental meta-rule), defines: practical study (pay regardless of everything else), links: refusal = mechanicalness (mechanical reaction). Pattern complete: negotiation question → denial/consequence → mechanism question → assertion/definition. They've shifted: payment = central (fundamental meta-rule), pay = regardless of everything else (assertion/definition).
visible_response:
The only way is to pay in advance, but how to do it is another thing—
all that we study here is how to pay in advance... Payment is a most important principle in the work, and it must be understood.
Without payment you cannot get anything.
But as a rule we want to get something for
nothing, and that is why we have nothing.
If we really decided to go for this kind of
knowledge—or even for quite a small thing—and we went for it regardless of
everything else, we would get it... We will pay for anything else, but for this we are not
prepared to pay anything, and so, as a result, we get nothing.
